key_insight: The paper introduces an adaptive multiscale two-phase flow solver that preserves physical conservation while updating coarse-scale spaces only when saturation-driven permeability changes become significant.
engineering_relevance: It enables accurate and efficient simulation of fluid displacement in high-contrast porous media, which is important for reservoir engineering, groundwater modeling, and subsurface transport prediction.
method: Couples a physics-preserving P-IMPES scheme with the mixed constraint energy minimizing generalized multiscale finite element method.
Monitors changes in mobility-weighted permeability and regenerates multiscale basis functions only when a tolerance is exceeded.
Uses local postprocessing to recover fine-grid mass conservation and improve phase-consistent fluxes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on multiple high-contrast permeability fields, showing that smaller adaptive tolerances improve saturation accuracy while reducing unnecessary multiscale updates.
watch_item: Performance depends on a prescribed update tolerance and a CFL condition, so overly aggressive coarsening or large time steps may degrade stability or accuracy.
paper_title: An Adaptive and Physics-Preserving Multiscale Method for Two-Phase Flow Simulations in High-Contrast Heterogeneous Porous Media
